Writes and reads are O(1) by hashing, averaging across K arrays to estimate neighbor statistics for error compensation; the sketch has a constant memory footprint, supports parallel updates, and is refreshed continuously during serving.
paper_title: ReLoop2: Building Self-Adaptive Recommendation Models via Responsive Error Compensation Loop

problem: Manual creation of interlinear glossed text (IGT) from field recordings is prohibitively slow, and there is no standardized, machine-readable speech+IGT resource spanning many low-resource languages.
method: Build FIELDWORK, the first multilingual speech-to-IGT dataset covering 37 languages with aligned audio, unsegmented transcription, underlying forms, glosses, and translations. Data are unified from multiple archives (ELAN/EXMARaLDA/Pangloss/NINJAL), converted to a standard schema (YAML, 16 kHz WAV), cleaned, and split by whole documents via a knapsack-based allocator to create realistic OOD dev/test splits; released as a Hugging Face dataset.
paper_title: WAV2GLOSS: Generating Interlinear Glossed Text from Speech

problem: There is no established approach to generate all IGT components directly from speech, especially across many low-resource languages.
method: Establish the WAV2GLOSS task and provide end-to-end baselines by fine-tuning pre-trained speech models: (a) self-supervised WavLM/XLS-R with frozen encoders plus a conformer encoder and transformer decoder trained with CTC-Attention and language/task tokens; and (b) supervised Whisper-style OWSM fully fine-tuned with BPE tokenizer and added task/language tokens. Benchmarks cover single- vs multi-task and mono- vs multilingual training across transcription, underlying, gloss, and translation outputs.
paper_title: WAV2GLOSS: Generating Interlinear Glossed Text from Speech

problem: It is unclear whether cascading ASR into text models outperforms end-to-end speech models for predicting glosses and translations under low-resource, multilingual conditions.
method: Evaluate a cascaded pipeline that feeds best ASR (XLS-R) transcriptions into ByT5 for underlying/gloss/translation, with and without pretraining on noisy IGT (ODIN). The study yields actionable insights: pre-trained decoder vocabularies benefit gloss/translation (OWSM), single-task generally beats multi-task, cascades improve translation but suffer from error propagation for gloss/underlying, and multilingual training helps the lowest-resource languages but degrades others (curse of multilinguality).
paper_title: WAV2GLOSS: Generating Interlinear Glossed Text from Speech

problem: Diffusion-based text-to-audio models achieve high fidelity but suffer from slow inference due to multi-step denoising with heavy attention backbones, while discrete-token mask-based models are faster but lower fidelity.
method: IMPACT performs iterative mask-based parallel decoding directly in a continuous latent space with a latent diffusion model: a Transformer latent encoder conditions on text+partial latents, and a lightweight MLP diffusion head (no heavy attention) predicts diffusion noise for masked positions over iterations. This preserves diffusion fidelity and delivers near-MAGNET latency by replacing heavy diffusion backbones and decoding many latents in parallel.
paper_title: IMPACT: Iterative Mask-based Parallel Decoding for Text-to-Audio Generation with Diffusion Modeling

problem: Conditional training must learn both audio generation and text alignment, and large paired text–audio datasets are scarce.
method: Introduce an unconditional pre-training phase (mask-based generative modeling without text) on large unlabeled audio to learn robust audio priors/reconstruction of masked latents, followed by text-conditional training (concatenated CLAP + Flan-T5 embeddings). This two-stage regimen substantially improves fidelity (FD/FAD) and text relevance while leveraging unlabeled corpora like AudioSet.
paper_title: IMPACT: Iterative Mask-based Parallel Decoding for Text-to-Audio Generation with Diffusion Modeling

problem: Confidence-guided token selection used in discrete MGM cannot be applied to continuous latents, risking unstable or slow iterative decoding.
method: Design a continuous-space decoding scheme with random position selection and a cosine mask schedule that progressively unmasks more latents each iteration, combined with classifier-free guidance scheduling. This enables stable, fast parallel decoding without confidence scores and supports a favorable quality–latency trade-off.
paper_title: IMPACT: Iterative Mask-based Parallel Decoding for Text-to-Audio Generation with Diffusion Modeling

problem: AVSR models trained with video-frame dropout become robust to missing video but degrade on complete inputs, and can even resemble unimodal ASR behavior without a clear explanation.
method: Diagnose and formalize dropout-induced modality bias via quantitative analyses (CER trends, transcription similarity, and latent-representation similarity) and propose the Modality Bias Hypothesis (MBH), which decomposes decisive features into audio-specific, visual-specific, and modality-general components to explain how video dropout shifts multimodal representations toward an audio-dominant unimodal distribution.
paper_title: A Study of Dropout-Induced Modality Bias on Robustness to Missing Video Frames for Audio-Visual Speech Recognition

problem: How to achieve robustness to missing video frames without sacrificing accuracy on complete audio-visual inputs in AVSR.
method: Multimodal Distribution Approximation with Knowledge Distillation (MDA-KD): train a teacher on complete pairs and a student with video dropout; distill at hidden layers to align the student’s latent distribution with the teacher’s multimodal distribution, anchoring against shift to audio-only and leveraging dual cross-attention to infer missing content from context and the complete modality.
paper_title: A Study of Dropout-Induced Modality Bias on Robustness to Missing Video Frames for Audio-Visual Speech Recognition

problem: Maintaining strong performance and efficiency when the video modality is severely or entirely missing, where synergistic fusion becomes unreliable.
method: Modality-Specific Adapter (MS-Adapter): insert LoRA-based adapters in the audio branch to dynamically switch decision-making to an audio-dominant pathway, enabling training with extra unimodal data and reducing computation by bypassing the video branch at inference.
paper_title: A Study of Dropout-Induced Modality Bias on Robustness to Missing Video Frames for Audio-Visual Speech Recognition

problem: Multilingual ASR for Indonesian and local languages lacks a unified benchmark and guidance on effective training strategies under low-resource conditions.
method: Assemble ~800 hours of ASR data across 10 languages and introduce NusaASR (17 datasets, 8 languages). Compare single-task, monolingual multi-task, and multilingual multi-task fine-tuning of XLS-R wav2vec 2.0 (pretrained and ASR-finetuned) and Whisper, showing multilingual multi-task training yields the lowest WER (~20% avg) and reveals cross-language transfer limits (e.g., poor transfer to Buginese).
paper_title: NusaCrowd: Open Source Initiative for Indonesian NLP Resources

problem: Discrete-token TTS systems are typically autoregressive and can only generate left-to-right, making them unsuitable for speech editing that requires both preceding and following context; reliance on acoustic tokens (RVQ) adds prediction complexity and codec-imposed quality limits.
method: CTX-txt2vec with contextual VQ-diffusion: a non-AR discrete diffusion model that generates semantic tokens from text while conditioning on both-side semantic context by feeding [cA, xt, cB] with indicator embeddings into a Transformer-based decoder; duration-aligned text encodings are added to self-attention outputs (no cross-attention), and training randomly mixes A/B/no-context cases to learn seamless concatenation for both continuation and editing.
paper_title: UniCATS: A Unified Context-Aware Text-to-Speech Framework with Contextual VQ-Diffusion and Vocoding

problem: Vocoder pipelines from semantic tokens struggle to capture speaker identity (e.g., x-vectors bottleneck) or depend on acoustic tokens/codecs that cap audio quality; need speaker-aware, high-fidelity resynthesis without speaker embeddings or codec tokens.
method: CTX-vec2wav with contextual vocoding: a Conformer-based vocoder that converts semantic tokens to waveforms while conditioning on mel-spectrogram prompts via cross-attention (mel encoded by a 1D-conv "mel encoder"); includes an auxiliary feature adaptor (pitch/energy/POV) for improved naturalness; trained by splitting utterances into a short mel prompt and a target segment, allowing variable-length mel prompts at inference to control speaker identity and achieving superior resynthesis quality over HifiGAN/AudioLM.
paper_title: UniCATS: A Unified Context-Aware Text-to-Speech Framework with Contextual VQ-Diffusion and Vocoding

problem: Rule-based text normalization for TTS is costly to build/maintain across many languages and fails on context-dependent ambiguities (e.g., dates, numerals, acronyms, mixed-domain tokens).
method: PolyNorm: a prompt-only, multilingual LLM-assisted TN system using a language-agnostic instruction template plus localized in-context examples; few-shot ICL guides normalization without handcrafted rules or fine-tuning, and an error-analysis–driven hill-climbing loop refines examples to fix systematic failures.
paper_title: PolyNorm: Few-Shot LLM-Based Text Normalization for Text-to-Speech

problem: There is no clean, comparable multilingual benchmark covering diverse TN phenomena, and MT-derived pairs are low quality/inconsistent across languages.
method: PolyNorm-Benchmark: multilingual TN datasets for 8 languages across 27 categories, created by generating unnormalized–normalized pairs with DeepSeek-R1 then expert editing/verification, using consistent taxonomies and language-specific orthographic conventions; released for reproducible evaluation.
paper_title: PolyNorm: Few-Shot LLM-Based Text Normalization for Text-to-Speech

problem: Evaluating TN fairly across diverse scripts and orthographic variants is difficult and can conflate tokenization with true normalization errors.
method: A language-agnostic curation and evaluation pipeline: standardized normalization conventions; acceptance rules that ignore non-phonetic differences (e.g., casing, optional spacing, ß/ss); WER/BLEU for whitespace languages and CER for non-whitespace languages, enabling scalable automatic benchmarking.
paper_title: PolyNorm: Few-Shot LLM-Based Text Normalization for Text-to-Speech

problem: Cascaded or parallel diarization+ASR systems misalign speaker indices with recognized words and struggle with overlapping speech and long meetings without a speaker inventory.
method: DNCASR: a unified architecture with two encoders (wav encoder for high-resolution acoustic features, speaker encoder for window-level embeddings) and two linked decoders (ASR and neural clustering) connected via a cross-attention 'link' that conditions the clustering decoder on ASR token-level hidden states, enabling joint end-to-end training under a single loss and producing serialized words with consistent meeting-wide speaker indices.
paper_title: DNCASR: End-to-End Training for Speaker-Attributed ASR

problem: Processing entire meetings exceeds ASR memory limits and prevents meeting-wide alignment of speaker indices across segments.
method: A two-stage joint fine-tuning regimen: (Stage 1) jointly train ASR and clustering with the link on current segments, predicting speaker indices up to the current segment; (Stage 2) freeze ASR, precompute token-level features for all segments, and fine-tune clustering to attend to all tokens meeting-wide, enabling single-pass decoding and improved speaker assignment in overlaps.
paper_title: DNCASR: End-to-End Training for Speaker-Attributed ASR

problem: Limited real meeting data causes overfitting; standard Diaconis augmentation can over-rotate speaker embeddings and hurt clustering.
method: Constrained Diaconis Augmentation (CDA) that limits the rotation angle of random orthogonal transforms applied to speaker embeddings, expanding training data while preserving embedding geometry, improving cpWER—especially on multi-talker segments.
paper_title: DNCASR: End-to-End Training for Speaker-Attributed ASR

problem: Convert monaural video audio to binaural while localizing sound sources without extensive ground-truth binaural labels or explicit visual annotations.
method: Introduce a self-supervised audio-visual consistency objective: compute audio–visual co-attention by cosine similarity between latent audio spectrogram patches and visual feature maps, apply left/right spatial weighting functions to infer per time–frequency left-right likelihoods from visuals, and align them via BCE to interaural level difference–based probabilities derived from (predicted or GT) left/right spectrogram magnitudes. This learns left-right localization signals without requiring labeled spatial annotations and works with partial binaural supervision.
paper_title: Exploiting Audio-Visual Consistency with Partial Supervision for Spatial Audio Generation

problem: Generate high-quality binaural outputs from mono while effectively injecting visual cues and reducing dependence on fully supervised data.
method: Use a U-Net-based spatial audio synthesizer that predicts complex masks for left and right channels from a mono spectrogram, conditioned on ResNet-extracted visual features. Train it jointly with a binaural reconstruction loss and the proposed audio-visual consistency loss, yielding improved binaural quality over prior methods and enabling semi-supervised training (competitive performance with substantially fewer labeled binaural samples).
paper_title: Exploiting Audio-Visual Consistency with Partial Supervision for Spatial Audio Generation

problem: Live multilingual videoconferencing needs low-latency captions that remain readable, but streaming ASR→MT with re-translation causes caption flicker and cognitive load.
method: A modular cascaded streaming ASR→MT system with anti-flicker and latency-control strategies tailored to on-screen captions: biased MT beam search that prefers consistency with prior outputs, translate-k/translate-t throttling, mask-k to delay unstable tail tokens, preserved line breaks, and smooth pixel-wise scrolling; plug-and-play ASR/MT backends.
paper_title: MeetDot: Videoconferencing with Live Translation Captions

problem: Standard MT metrics miss user-critical aspects of streaming captions (when text first appears, how often it changes, and how bursty updates are).
method: An integrated evaluation suite that computes intrinsic, caption-sequence–based metrics: accuracy (Final BLEU), latency (Translation lag), stability (Normalized erasure), and newly introduced measures—Initial lag, Incremental caption lag, Mean/Max word burstiness—for hill-climbing and comparing ASR/MT configurations.
paper_title: MeetDot: Videoconferencing with Live Translation Captions

problem: There is no practical extrinsic metric to compare end-to-end communication quality across captioning strategies in live settings.
method: A cross-lingual, Taboo-style word-guessing game embedded in the system with automatic scoring to A/B test captioning conditions; higher game scores indicate faster, more accurate, and more readable live translation.
paper_title: MeetDot: Videoconferencing with Live Translation Captions

problem: Text-to-audio generators lack faithful, causal explanations of how specific text tokens influence generated audio; attention rollouts are non-causal and single-token perturbations miss inter-token interactions.
method: AudioGenX learns a soft importance mask over cross-attention that perturbs multiple text tokens simultaneously and treats the mask itself as the explanation. An MLP explainer with sigmoid + Gumbel-softmax predicts the mask, optimized by complementary factual (preserve the original prediction) and counterfactual (change the prediction) objectives measured via cosine similarity in the latent audio-token space.
paper_title: AudioGenX: Explainability on Text-to-Audio Generative Models

problem: Generating explanations over long audio sequences is computationally expensive and users often need explanations at whole-clip or interval-level granularity.
method: Reformulate explanation at the audio-token level to enable parallel optimization per token, then aggregate masks across tokens or user-specified time windows to obtain holistic or granular explanations. This yields linear complexity and reduced memory usage while preserving fidelity.
paper_title: AudioGenX: Explainability on Text-to-Audio Generative Models

problem: There is no standardized way to evaluate the faithfulness of explanations for text-to-audio generation.
method: Introduce an evaluation protocol using an external audio classifier (PaSST) to compute factual/counterfactual Fidelity and KL-divergence metrics by comparing classifier outputs on original vs masked generations, enabling quantitative benchmarking against Transformer XAI baselines.
paper_title: AudioGenX: Explainability on Text-to-Audio Generative Models

problem: Bridging the reality gap to transfer vision-based models trained in simulation to real robots without any real-image data or fine-tuning.
method: Domain randomization: train solely on simulated RGB by heavily randomizing textures (algorithmically generated non-photorealistic), lighting, camera pose/FOV, distractors, and image noise so the real world appears as another variation; no calibration or real-image pretraining required.
paper_title: Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World

problem: Achieving precise object localization from a single, uncalibrated monocular image in clutter/occlusion to enable manipulation.
method: Train a VGG-16–based CNN regressor in simulation to predict object Cartesian coordinates assuming fixed table height (2D pose), with randomized scenes; achieves ~1.5 cm real-world accuracy and enables grasping in clutter via MoveIt! (38/40 successes) and transfer to real textured objects (YCB Spam can).
paper_title: Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World

problem: Determining which aspects of randomization and training enable successful sim-to-real transfer and reduce reliance on pretrained features.
method: Ablation study varying dataset size, number of unique textures, distractors, camera randomization, noise, and ImageNet pretraining; finds >1,000 unique textures and including distractors are critical, camera randomization provides gains, and pretraining is unnecessary with enough simulated data.
paper_title: Domain Randomization for Transferring Deep Neural Networks from Simulation to the Real World

problem: Predicting human behavior for HRI requires capturing inherently multimodal responses at ~1s timescales and conditioning on both interaction history and the robot’s planned actions.
method: Learn p(u_h future | history, u_r future) with a Conditional Variational Autoencoder that uses a discrete latent variable z to represent high-level human response modes (e.g., yield vs. pass) and an autoregressive LSTM with GMM outputs to capture within-mode variability across the prediction horizon; training employs scheduled sampling and conditions explicitly on candidate robot futures for response-aware prediction.
paper_title: Multimodal Probabilistic Model-Based Planning for Human-Robot Interaction

problem: Selecting robot actions in real time under multimodal human uncertainty demands efficient, transparent model-based planning over short horizons.
method: Perform exhaustive, massively parallel evaluation of discrete robot action sequences by sampling human responses from the learned CVAE model; use a two-stage sampling scheme (coarse scoring across all sequences, then deep resampling of top candidates) and apply the best sequence in an MPC loop, enabling ~100k human-future simulations every 0.3s on a GPU and interpretable, anticipation-driven behaviors in traffic weaving.
paper_title: Multimodal Probabilistic Model-Based Planning for Human-Robot Interaction

problem: Sampling-based motion planners struggle in environments with narrow passages and lack a way to reuse experience across different but locally similar workspaces.
method: Decompose the workspace into reusable local primitives (e.g., pairs of obstacles). For each primitive, generate multiple local planning paths that traverse difficult regions and fit a local sampler as a GMM (initially one Gaussian per useful configuration, equal weights, fixed covariance). Store the sampler parameters keyed by the primitive’s geometric descriptor in a database for retrieval and incremental growth.
paper_title: Using Local Experiences for Global Motion Planning

problem: How to convert fragmented local experiences into an effective global sampling bias without committing to past full paths or requiring complex learned models.
method: Global-Local Sampler (GL-Sampler): detect local primitives in the query workspace, retrieve the closest local samplers via a similarity function (or create if missing), optionally transform them via robot symmetries to match the current instance, and synthesize a global sampler by mixing the local samplers with weights proportional to their mixture counts; combine with uniform sampling (controlled by λ) and plug into PRM/RRT without algorithmic changes.
paper_title: Using Local Experiences for Global Motion Planning

problem: Large, redundant local samplers slow online retrieval and inflate storage.
method: Two compactions: (a) connectivity-aware clique merging—build a graph over sampled configurations with straight-line collision-free edges, greedily find cliques, and replace each with a single Gaussian (mean/cov) to preserve C-space connectivity while reducing mixtures; (b) symmetry-based transformations—apply known robot symmetries (e.g., base rotation) to map stored samplers to new primitives, shrinking the effective primitive space and database size.
paper_title: Using Local Experiences for Global Motion Planning

problem: General video-to-command captioning focuses on whole-frame context and misses manipulated object changes, leading to incorrect commands for robotic pick-and-place.
method: A video captioning network (CNet) that fuses global frame CNN features with a local object-centric Video Difference Map computed from start/end-frame color masks, then encoded via a seq2seq LSTM with modified input units to jointly model both feature types.
paper_title: Learning Actions from Human Demonstration Video for Robotic Manipulation

problem: Robots need reliable grasp poses and object-aware cues to both execute commands and provide object-focused context for captioning.
method: A grasp detection network (GNet) combining SegNet-style RGBD segmentation and CNN classification; from object masks it computes convex hulls and minimum-area rectangles to output grasp center/orientation and category, and produces illumination-robust color masks; trained end-to-end with a multi-task loss.
paper_title: Learning Actions from Human Demonstration Video for Robotic Manipulation

problem: Translating human demonstration videos into executable, reliable robotic actions in real settings.
method: An integrated two-module pipeline where CNet generates grammar-free commands mapped to robot actions while GNet supplies grasp solutions and object-focused features, validated on a UR5 across stacking/placing tasks with large gains in captioning metrics and real-world success rates.
paper_title: Learning Actions from Human Demonstration Video for Robotic Manipulation

problem: Accurate motion prediction for rigid bodies with planar non-convex contact patches is hard; point-contact or ad hoc multi-point approximations introduce errors and can allow penetration.
method: A geometrically implicit time-stepping formulation (MNCP) that models the non-convex contact via the convex hull of the object, and simultaneously solves for the equivalent contact points (ECPs), contact impulses, and state using KKT-based contact constraints; proves no artificial penetration even if the ECP lies outside the physical patch.
paper_title: Rigid Body Motion Prediction with Planar Non-convex Contact Patch

problem: Robotic manipulation requires seamless handling of intermittent contacts and transitions among patch, line, and point contacts without manual mode enumeration.
method: Couple collision detection with dynamics so contact geometry (ECPs and normals) is solved at the end of each step; apply maximum power dissipation (elliptic Coulomb) friction at the ECP, enabling automatic, consistent transitions between contact modes within a single unified MNCP.
paper_title: Rigid Body Motion Prediction with Planar Non-convex Contact Patch

problem: Existing non-convex models using unions of convex patches are computationally heavy and yield non-unique force/moment distributions; sliding-only models are too restrictive.
method: Replace multi-patch modeling with a single convex-hull-based contact constraint, discretized with Euler and solved as an MNCP; empirically matches analytic/sliding models while being significantly faster than union-of-convex MNCPs and robust across desk and T-bar simulations.
paper_title: Rigid Body Motion Prediction with Planar Non-convex Contact Patch

problem: Safely and effectively shaping elasto-plastic solids (e.g., dough) is hard with rigid tools and complex to model due to nonlinear deformations.
method: Design a low-cost elastic end-effector for a parallel-jaw gripper whose stiffness is continuously varied by gripper throw; apply controlled back-and-forth rolling against a surface. The same variable-stiffness tool is also usable for active palpation with an overhead depth camera to sense deformation, enabling stiffness-aware manipulation.
paper_title: Deformable Elasto-Plastic Object Shaping using an Elastic Hand and Model-Based Reinforcement Learning

problem: Planning efficient action sequences to achieve a target dough length under unknown, complex elastoplastic dynamics with limited data.
method: Model-based reinforcement learning with a task-specific, low-dimensional state (length, width, height, highest-point xy) extracted from depth, and a compressed action space (elastic-band stretch and indentation depth; roll distance tied to stretch; roll centered at the dough’s highest point) for tractable MPC. Learn a transition model from ~1 hour of real robot random exploration using linear regression with polynomial features, and plan via MPC using CEM or Powell’s method, yielding up to ~60% fewer actions than a heuristic.
paper_title: Deformable Elasto-Plastic Object Shaping using an Elastic Hand and Model-Based Reinforcement Learning

problem: Transferring to doughs with different stiffness (hydration) without retraining from scratch or suffering from poor model initialization.
method: Estimate dough stiffness by calibrating force as a function of band stretch and indentation depth using the elastic hand, then initialize dynamics via stiffness-weighted interpolation between models trained on soft and stiff dough. Combine with online model updates in MPC, improving efficiency by ~40% over incorrect initialization.
paper_title: Deformable Elasto-Plastic Object Shaping using an Elastic Hand and Model-Based Reinforcement Learning

problem: Manipulation policies trained on all sensed variables are brittle under domain shifts and costly to adapt; the robot lacks a way to identify which state variables are actually relevant for a task.
method: CREST performs causal interventions in an internal (approximate) simulator: optimize a policy on a sampled context, then single-variable intervene and test whether the policy still succeeds; variables that break success are marked relevant (τ). Policies are then built as Reduced MLPs that take only τ as input, pretrained with domain randomization over τ, and transferred for fine-tuning in the target domain.
paper_title: Causal Reasoning in Simulation for Structure and Transfer Learning of Robot Manipulation Policies

problem: Different controller parameters may depend on different subsets of state variables; learning a monolithic mapping wastes capacity and harms generalization.
method: After identifying τ, CREST applies interventions only on τ, re-solves the task, and searches for a minimal set of parameter changes (approximate L0 via breadth-first search). The intervened variable is assigned to τ_j for parameters that needed to change. This yields a per-parameter structural causal model implemented as a Partitioned MLP with one head per parameter conditioned only on its τ_j.
paper_title: Causal Reasoning in Simulation for Structure and Transfer Learning of Robot Manipulation Policies

problem: Sim-to-real transfer suffers from distribution shifts in irrelevant features and dynamics; policies need to scale with task complexity while remaining robust and sample-efficient to adapt.
method: A structure-based sim-to-real pipeline: use CREST to prune inputs, pretrain with PPO using domain randomization limited to relevant variables, then transfer and fine-tune. This yields smaller networks that scale with relevant context size and are, by construction, robust to shifts in irrelevant variables and more sample-efficient under dynamics shifts, enabling improved zero-shot and fine-tuning performance on block stacking and crate opening, including real-robot block stacking.
paper_title: Causal Reasoning in Simulation for Structure and Transfer Learning of Robot Manipulation Policies

problem: Real-time, robust 6-DoF tracking of previously unseen objects under occlusion/noise without requiring 3D object models or per-object training.
method: Combine SiamMask with THOR’s long-/short-term template management for 2D RGB tracking and segmentation, then register a template point cloud (captured at initialization) to the segmented depth observation each frame via Generalized-ICP; use voxel filtering for speed and a kinematic prior when the robot moves the object. This class-agnostic, model-free pipeline (THOR + G-ICP) mitigates drift and handles occlusion.
paper_title: Deep 6-DoF Tracking of Unknown Objects for Reactive Grasping

problem: Selecting and updating feasible grasp poses for unknown objects in real time while accounting for robot kinematics, collisions, and scene/object dynamics.
method: A dynamic grasp configuration observer generates N candidates by solving IK with weighted task costs (object-relative position, end-effector–object axis alignment, collision avoidance, joint limits/homing). It injects a randomized alignment term via a random orthonormal basis with age-dependent weights to diversify approach axes, then ranks candidates by whether the end-effector lies inside the object cuboid and proximity to its center; candidates are re-evaluated continuously.
paper_title: Deep 6-DoF Tracking of Unknown Objects for Reactive Grasping

problem: Planning collision-free approach motions in dynamic environments where pre-planned paths fail due to object or obstacle motion.
method: A reactive 'backstepping' planner follows a straight-line target trajectory from homing q0 to the selected grasp q*. At each step it moves toward the closest point on the target path; upon predicted collision it temporarily steps backward toward q0 before re-advancing, enabling fast online replanning in changing scenes.
paper_title: Deep 6-DoF Tracking of Unknown Objects for Reactive Grasping

problem: LLM-based robot planners lack explicit object-kinematic understanding, limiting generalization across diverse articulated objects and preventing low-level control grounded in physical constraints.
method: Unified Kinematic Knowledge Parser: detect/segment object parts and joints, estimate joint properties (type, axis/orientation) and contact points (via simulator or a perception stack such as Grounding-DINO + SAM + GAPartNet), and serialize them into a unified XML-like textual description. This standardized, code-like kinematic prompt lets the LLM parse and reason over heterogeneous articulated structures in a common format.
paper_title: Kinematic-aware Prompting for Generalizable Articulated Object Manipulation with LLMs

problem: Translating abstract manipulation commonsense into precise, kinematically-feasible 3D control trajectories with minimal robotic data, especially for complex revolute motions.
method: Kinematic-aware Manipulation Planner: a hierarchical, chain-of-thought prompting scheme that (1) generates an abstract manipulation sequence explicitly grounded in the kinematic description by copying referenced properties (coordinates, joint axes), then (2) produces precise 3D waypoints using a constrained action grammar via in-context learning from only 17 waypoint demonstrations. Waypoints are executed with an operational space controller, yielding zero-shot generalization to unseen articulated categories and accurate trajectories for both prismatic and revolute joints.
paper_title: Kinematic-aware Prompting for Generalizable Articulated Object Manipulation with LLMs

problem: Large Sim2Real gap in end-to-end robot learning due to low photorealism, poor geometry, and mismatched lighting in Real2Sim assets, especially in complex scenes and with non-Lambertian/thin structures.
method: A hierarchical Real2Sim pipeline using 3D Gaussian Splatting as a universal visual representation: (i) lidar-assisted geometry-regularized scene reconstruction for robust background 3DGS; (ii) HDR environment lighting estimation via DiffusionLight for scene-consistent illumination; (iii) object-level assets from laser-scanned meshes or single-image 3D generation (CLAY) with offline PBR relighting; and (iv) a Mesh→Gaussian transfer that initializes one slanted planar Gaussian per mesh face (barycenter placement, normal-aligned frames, scale from face geometry) with depth/opacity regularization, yielding a dual 3DGS–Mesh representation (photorealistic rendering + accurate physics).
paper_title: DISCOVERSE: Efficient Robot Simulation in Complex High-Fidelity Environments

problem: Need for a unified simulator that combines photorealistic rendering, accurate physics, multi-sensor fidelity, and high throughput with easy integration into robotics stacks.
method: Unified engine integrating a CUDA-optimized, tile-based 3DGS renderer with MuJoCo physics and ROS2; native multi-sensor simulation including RGB-D via Gaussian alpha compositing and a BVH-accelerated Gaussian ray tracer for LiDAR (>100 FPS), plus tactile via Tacchi; supports diverse robots/assets/ROS plugins and achieves high throughput (e.g., 650 FPS for 5 RGB-D cameras).
paper_title: DISCOVERSE: Efficient Robot Simulation in Complex High-Fidelity Environments

problem: Slow, costly demonstration collection and lack of evidence that photorealistic simulation reliably improves zero-shot Sim2Real transfer.
method: Automated state generation and motion-planner/gamepad keypointing for rapid demo capture (~100× faster than real-world collection), combined with image and generative randomization (video overlays/HSV/gamma; ControlNet- and GPT-4V–aided augmentation, flow-based interpolation). Training ACT and Diffusion Policy in DISCOVERSE yields state-of-the-art zero-shot Sim2Real success, surpassing SplatSim and mesh-based simulators without/with augmentation.
paper_title: DISCOVERSE: Efficient Robot Simulation in Complex High-Fidelity Environments

problem: Robots need to predict not only geometric deformation but also topological changes (splitting, merging, self-merging) of elastoplastic objects from partial RGB-D and planned tool motions, especially over long horizons.
method: DoughNet, a Transformer-based visual predictive model that encodes a partial point cloud into a fixed-size set of latent codes representing object components; a decoder outputs per-component occupancy masks and a genus classifier, while a topology-aware dynamics module performs autoregressive set-to-set prediction entirely in latent space conditioned on the manipulator’s surface geometry across consecutive timesteps. This joint, component-wise implicit representation enables accurate long-horizon geometry and topology prediction without post-hoc checks.
paper_title: DoughNet: A Visual Predictive Model for Topological Manipulation of Deformable Objects

problem: Training topology-aware models is hindered by the absence of reliable ground-truth dynamic connectivity (merges/splits) in particle-based simulators.
method: A synthetic data generator with dynamic topology-checking operations in an MPM simulator: perturb candidate regions opposite/orthogonal to closing direction to test merges/splits, updating a scene-topology graph (components and cycles/genus). It outputs per-component meshes via Dual Contouring and renders noisy single-view RGB-D, providing supervision for geometry, component segmentation, and genus prediction.
paper_title: DoughNet: A Visual Predictive Model for Topological Manipulation of Deformable Objects

problem: Planning actions and tool selection to achieve target topologies and geometries from partial observations is challenging.
method: Use DoughNet as a forward model within a CEM planner: roll out sampled manipulator geometries/poses/opening widths and score predictions via cosine similarity in the learned latent space (capturing geometry and topology). This enables selecting the tool and action parameters that realize robot- or human-defined topological goals, with successful sim-to-real transfer.
paper_title: DoughNet: A Visual Predictive Model for Topological Manipulation of Deformable Objects

problem: Robotic manipulation requires accurate metric depth, but monocular depth estimation models output only affine-invariant depths and can invert/localize depths incorrectly, making them unsuitable for grasping without depth sensors.
method: MOMA with Scale-Shift-Rotation Alignment (SSRA): perform a one-shot calibration using tens–hundreds of sparse ground-truth depth points; treat MDEM output with pseudo intrinsics and solve a non-linear optimization over scale, 3D rotation, and shift to directly minimize depth alignment error. Assumes a fixed camera, needs no retraining, runs in milliseconds at inference, and substantially reduces MAE/boosts δ metrics for grasping.
paper_title: Monocular One-Shot Metric-Depth Alignment for RGB-Based Robot Grasping

problem: One-shot calibration fails if the MDEM’s raw depth range fluctuates across scenes despite a fixed camera, breaking transfer of alignment parameters.
method: Depth normalization of MDEM outputs (min–max normalization) before fitting and at inference to stabilize scale/shift across scenes, enabling reliable reuse of the one-shot SSRA parameters and outperforming median or no normalization in ablations.
paper_title: Monocular One-Shot Metric-Depth Alignment for RGB-Based Robot Grasping

problem: Transparent objects defeat commodity depth sensors and degrade off-the-shelf MDEMs, limiting grasping in real settings.
method: Fine-tune a strong MDEM (Depth Anything) with LoRA on large transparent-object datasets and couple it with MOMA/SSRA; this combination generalizes to transparent objects and achieves high success rates in two-finger and suction-based grasping without using a depth sensor.
paper_title: Monocular One-Shot Metric-Depth Alignment for RGB-Based Robot Grasping
